pages.full_text:
up to Sospel.
	The town Sospel is lovely, old + new. An old bridge with a building on it. We are in the municipal park camping place, only 2 loos + 2 showers but very clean. It's an odd assortment of campers. We are next to 2 town drunks in a low tent; v. beaten-up face, chain smoking, slumped against a tree, 7 men who are probaly cycling + several families.
	We walked into the Town + paid £3.50 to use the internet for 20 minutes, to see how Charles is (fine) bought bread + veg + wandered around.
	The plan is we will take a train to Menton tomorrow, via Nice, then Monaco + spend the night in Monaco + see the home of Miss Rothschild on the way back. We have received our place for 3 nights. We have run down the food + will switch off the fridge.
pages.notes: Page starts mid-sentence ('up to Sospel.') — top of page is the continuation of text from the previous spread. No handwritten page number visible on this side. 'probaly' is the diary's spelling. 'Miss Rothschild' refers to Villa Ephrussi de Rothschild, mentioned more fully on the right page.

pages.full_text:
Thursday We caught the train → Nice → Menton + wandered round, climbed the hill (exhausting) + had lunch. There were lots of rugby signs in the street. Faded grandeur, gentle, has-been. Old houses at one end of the sea front, which Simon could remember. Nice shops. Promenade
We trained to Monaco where Tourist Information told us that all cheap rooms were already let, so we'd have to stay in an expensive hotel. WHOOPEE! We got a balcony + bath. We swam in the hotel pool. It was really nice, the Colombus.
We picked up supper in a delicatessen - meat balls + spinach pie + went into the Grimaldi forum for the Princess Grace exhibition. She was stunningly [?beaten] beautiful. The son looks nice, Prince Albert.
After a large breakfast we took the train back to Beaulieu sur Mer. The T. Information said it was short walk - 800m … well it took over half-an-hour. (Disagreement here). Finally we reached Villa Ephrussi de Rothschild. It's spectacular, painted in pink, Beatrice's favourite colour.
She bought 14 acres at the narrow part of the isthmus of Cape Ferrat + built a dream house, filled with art she had collected from all over the world. There are views of the sea all round
pages.notes: Page number 64 visible top-right. 'Colombus' is the diary's spelling of the hotel (Hotel Columbus Monaco). One word before 'beautiful' is difficult to read — possibly 'beaten' used as an intensifier/superlative (struck through and replaced with 'beautiful'), or possibly a crossed-out word. Transcribed as [?beaten]. 'Prince Albert' and 'Princess Grace' are named. 'T. Information' = Tourist Information. Text ends mid-sentence ('all round') continuing on next spread. No year explicitly stated on this page; context (South of France, Monaco, Côte d'Azur trip, internet café mention, volume B covering 2003–2019) suggests early-to-mid 2000s but year cannot be confirmed from this page alone.
